data.narrative: Student demonstrated a high level of maturity in transforming toxic feedback into actionable mentorship. They correctly prioritized a runtime crash (division-by-zero) over stylistic preferences while maintaining a respectful tone that encourages team velocity.

dimension_scores.observation: Student explicitly separated blockers from suggestions and used questions to drive refactoring decisions.
dimension_scores.evidence: Rewrote 'Refactor this whole thing' into a suggestion to use a helper function for testability.

dimension_scores.observation: Feedback was concise and categorized (Bug, Naming, Iteration, Nesting).
dimension_scores.evidence: Student used headers to structure the review into scannable sections.

dimension_scores.observation: Every negative point was paired with a code example or a specific path forward.
dimension_scores.evidence: Provided a guard for the division-by-zero bug: 'avg_age = total_age / cnt if cnt > 0 else 0'.

strengths: Expert-level ability to categorize feedback by severity.
Strong use of 'why' before 'what' to explain technical debt.
growth_areas: Continue applying this severity signaling to all forms of communication.
data.recommendation: Maintain the Bug vs Suggestion headers in real-world reviews to avoid PR gridlock.

public_evidence.artifact: PR Review for Authentication Service (Scenario B)
public_evidence.student_action: Identified and corrected an email enumeration vulnerability and a division-by-zero runtime bug.
public_evidence.technical_detail: Auth code review involving email enumeration risk and Python runtime safety.
public_evidence.proof: Rewrote toxic feedback into structured, severity-labeled PR comments with code examples.
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Proves the student can protect system security and reliability while mentoring junior engineers.
